commit.message:
Add MXFP8 XPU feasibility analysis

Document the investigation into MXFP8 support for Intel XPU via sycl-tla:
- Survey of all Xe dispatch policies in sycl-tla (gap: no grouped+FP8+scaling)
- float_ue8m0_t support (software fallback only on Intel Xe; no CUDA PTX)
- Intel Xe hardware limits (no native blockscaled MXFP8 XMX instruction)
- Three implementation options with effort/quality tradeoffs
- Recommendation: Option A (software dequant pre-pass) now, Option B
  (new sycl-tla MainloopXeL1StagedGroupFP8Scaling) as upstream contribution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use importlib.metadata to read package version

Reading VERSION via a relative path from __file__ breaks when the
package is installed (e.g. into site-packages) because the VERSION
file is not shipped there. Switch to importlib.metadata.version(),
which reads the version from the installed package metadata instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Xuewei Niu <justxuewei@apache.org>

fix: restore qwen3.5:latest + LOCAL_MAX_TOKENS=512 on SintAItion

gemma4:e2b with LOCAL_MAX_TOKENS=256 caused circular/truncated answers
(e.g. 'вращение является результатом вращения'). Two root causes:
  1. LOCAL_MAX_TOKENS=256 too restrictive — forced model to loop when
     it ran out of space mid-explanation
  2. gemma4:e2b (2B effective params) too small for factual Russian Q&A

Fix applied to ~/.taris/bot.env on SintAItion:
  OLLAMA_MODEL=qwen3.5:latest  (9B, 100% benchmark score)
  LOCAL_MAX_TOKENS=512         (was 256)

Service restarted, Polling Telegram confirmed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pihole): route admin UI to single pod via stable node IP

The Traefik 'weighted' sticky cookie does not work at the pod level
when wrapping a K8s Service — kube-proxy still round-robins across
pods, so login sessions were created on one pod and validated on
another, causing a login loop.

Fix: Create pihole-web-primary Service + manual Endpoints pointing
to 10.0.69.10:30080 (control10 NodePort). With externalTrafficPolicy:
Local on the NodePort, this always reaches the pihole pod on control10.
Admin UI sessions are stable; DNS services still load-balance across
all 3 pods.

Also: restore oauth2-proxy-auth middleware (accidentally removed in
previous commit), drop the now-unneeded TraefikService.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Bump to v0.0.4, add .vscodeignore, remove stale vsix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): coerce undefined globals to 0 + pooled object creation

Promoted globals in the preheader (e.g. var temp;) start as undefined.
The smi_guarded CheckSmi was deopting the entire function because
undefined is not a Smi.  Since ToInt32(undefined) = 0, coerce non-Smi
promoted globals to 0 instead of deopting.  This prevents the
universal deopt that was causing ALL benchmarks to fall back to
the interpreter during measurement.

Also add Rc<RefCell<PropertyMap>> object pool that recycles allocations
for CreateObjectLiteral, eliminating per-object heap allocation on the
hot path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove StrictMode to prevent double WebSocket connections

React 18 StrictMode double-invokes effects in dev mode, creating two
WebSocket connections. The second one escapes the pause/disconnect
lifecycle. Removing StrictMode fixes the phantom sync channel.

Fix relay endpoint killing tunnel + NameError on log

Two bugs:

1. pollFlows() called /api/relay/dandroid1/api/flow/list every 5s,
   opening new tunnel connections on each call (separate from the
   remote_fetcher_loop fix). Fixed:
   - pollFlows interval: 5s → 30s
   - Relay endpoint returns cached data for /api/sessions and
     /api/flow/list if cache is <60s old, avoiding tunnel round-trip

2. NameError: 'log' not defined in api_relay error handlers (lines
   418, 432). Fixed: use local _log variable.

commit.message:
fix: prevent callee JIT deopt from propagating to caller

When a callee's Maglev or baseline JIT code deopts, the caller should
NOT be forced to deopt as well. Instead, the callee deopt should be
handled by marking the deopt, invalidating the JIT cache, and falling
through to the interpreter.

Fix call_undefined_receiver0_inner:
- Maglev path: on callee deopt, mark maglev deopted, invalidate cache,
  restore context, and fall through to baseline/interpreter.
- Baseline path: on callee deopt, mark baseline deopted, restore
  context, and fall through to interpreter.

Fix Maglev codegen (emit_direct_call_0/1/2):
- After JIT call returns JIT_DEOPT, instead of deopting the caller via
  emit_deopt_check_rax, fall back to the runtime stub which runs the
  interpreter and returns a valid result.
- Mono-hit path: reload callee from cache and call runtime stub.
- Cache-miss path: pop callee from stack and call runtime stub.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: repair stocked products by updating stock entries before product QU fields

The old approach (bridging conversions) failed because Grocy rejects QU
conversions referencing deleted units. New approach:
1. When product QU update fails due to stock constraint, fetch stock entries
2. Update each stock entry's qu_id to the new valid unit
3. Retry the product QU update (now succeeds because stock uses new QU)
4. Fall back to fixing non-stock fields individually if still failing

Also adds get_stock_entries() and update_stock_entry() to Grocy API client.
469 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Removed bold tags and bullet points.

commit.message:
Migrate simple-orders-app from Create React App to Vite (#90)

Replaced react-scripts build tooling with Vite for faster builds and
modern development experience.

## Changes

- **Build configuration**: Created `vite.config.js` with React plugin,
HTTPS support, and port 3000 to match previous CRA setup
- **Project structure**: Moved `index.html` from `public/` to root (Vite
requirement), updated to use direct paths instead of `%PUBLIC_URL%`
tokens
- **Source files**: Renamed `.js` files containing JSX to `.jsx` for
proper Vite transformation (`index.js` → `index.jsx`, `App.js` →
`App.jsx`)
- **Scripts**: Replaced `start`/`test`/`eject` with
`dev`/`build`/`preview` commands
- **Dependencies**: Removed `react-scripts`, added `vite` and
`@vitejs/plugin-react` as dev dependencies
- **Configuration cleanup**: Removed CRA-specific package.json fields
(`eslintConfig`, `browserslist`, `overrides`)

## Development

```bash
npm run dev      # Start dev server at https://localhost:3000
npm run build    # Build to build/ directory
npm run preview  # Preview production build
```

The inline script loading the order component SDK remains unchanged and
functional.

---------

Co-authored-by: anthropic-code-agent[bot] <242468646+Claude@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: esbenbach <1378302+esbenbach@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: visualizer gets explicit 68px height to break 100% chain

The .viz-frame CSS uses height:100% which collapses to 0 when no ancestor
has a concrete height. self-stretch cannot override an explicit CSS height
property. Fixed by setting height:68px directly via style prop to match
the two toolbar rows (2×32px + 4px gap).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add scripts/run-local.sh to build and run container locally

Auto-detects podman or docker. Reads OPENHAB_URL and OPENHAB_TOKEN
from environment or .env.local file. Stops any existing container
with the same name before starting a fresh one.

Usage:
  OPENHAB_URL=https://... OPENHAB_TOKEN=oh.... ./scripts/run-local.sh
  # or create .env.local with those vars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Cache parsed remote JSON-LD contexts to avoid re-parsing on each edit

The converter was re-parsing fetched remote @context documents (e.g.
schema.org, ~300KB of JSON-LD) on every call to convert_with_loader.
The WasmFetchLoader cached the raw string but not the parsed result,
so every keystroke in the demo triggered a full parse of the 300KB
context document — the actual root cause of >1s latency.

Fix: thread-local PARSED_CTX_CACHE keyed by URL stores the parsed
JsonLdVal.  First access parses; subsequent accesses return the
cached value.  Zero signature changes to existing functions.

Also: make JsonLdVal public and expose clear_parsed_context_cache()
for cache invalidation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(hosted): custom response converter for multi-tool-call support

The default ResponseAPIMessagesNonStreamResponseConverter only emits the
first tool_call per AIMessage, silently dropping the rest. QPrisma's agent
routinely makes 2-3 parallel tool calls per turn, creating orphaned
ToolMessage outputs that Foundry rejects as 'invalid format'.

QPrismaNonStreamResponseConverter fixes this by:
- Emitting ALL FunctionToolCallItemResource per AIMessage
- Tracking emitted call_ids and dropping orphaned tool outputs
- Skipping restore_media_context node (returns conversation history, not output)
- Filtering HumanMessage/SystemMessage as defense-in-depth
- Handling AIMessage with both text content and tool_calls

Wired into QPrismaStateConverter via create_non_stream_response_converter
factory callback pattern.

17 new tests covering multi-tool, orphan guard, node filtering, HITL,
edge cases, and realistic QPrisma query flows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: P_Bayes always 1.0000 in integration layer due to per-pair normalization (#97)

* Initial plan

* fix: globally normalize P_Bayes in integration layer to prevent 1.0000 display

Root cause: _compute_bayesian_posteriors normalized within each (A,B) pair.
Since composition_table has exactly one C per pair, the per-pair normalization
always yielded probability=1.0, causing the integration formula to always show
P_Bayes=1.0000 — inconsistent with the probability tree (which normalized
across all transitions).

Fix:
- _compute_bayesian_posteriors now returns (weights_dict, Z_pair) — raw
  unnormalized weights rather than pre-divided probabilities.
- run_bayesian_inference accepts weights+Z_pair and stores partition_function
  as the true unnormalized Z, not sum(normalized probs).
- run_dual_inference uses a two-pass approach: collect all raw results, compute
  Z_global = sum of all posterior_weights, then normalize each probability as
  posterior_weight/Z_global before calling integrate(). This makes P_Bayes
  consistent with the probability tree display.
- confidence_final is now correctly bounded by P_Bayes (≤ P_Bayes always).

Tests:
- Updated existing tests to use new _compute_bayesian_posteriors/
  run_bayesian_inference signatures.
- Added regression tests: P_Bayes≠1.0 with multiple transitions,
  P_Bayes consistent with probability tree, confidence_final≤P_Bayes,
  partition_function equals Z_global.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wuqihang-brave/El-druin/sessions/70a67328-1076-46ee-ab4e-2063a9821c89

Co-authored-by: wuqihang-brave <269888202+wuqihang-brave@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract _NEAR_ZERO constant and unify tolerance to 1e-4 in tests

- Add _NEAR_ZERO = 1e-9 module-level constant replacing scattered magic literals
- Use _NEAR_ZERO consistently across all near-zero comparisons in the engine
- Unify floating-point tolerance to 1e-4 across all test assertions

feat(#28): add ingredient normalization backend (#127)

Implements POST /api/recipes/normalize-ingredients endpoint that calls
Gemini to detect ambiguous ingredient lines and suggest normalized
alternatives with confidence scoring.

BDD Scenarios:
- Scenario 1: Ambiguous ingredients → normalization suggestions returned
- Scenario 2: Low-confidence suggestions (< 0.6) → filtered out
- Scenario 3: Empty ingredient list → returns empty list gracefully
- Scenario 4: API failure → returns empty list (graceful degradation)

Changes:
- IngredientNormalizationRequest (new): request DTO with ingredients + recipeName
- IngredientNormalization (new): single suggestion with index/original/normalized/reason/confidence
- IngredientNormalizationResponse (new): response DTO wrapping list of normalizations
- IngredientNormalizationService (new): Gemini call, JSON schema, confidence filter
- RecipeController: 4th service arg + POST /normalize-ingredients endpoint
- RecipeControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- RefineInstructionsControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- SuggestFieldsControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- NormalizeIngredientsControllerTest (new): controller-level BDD tests
- IngredientNormalizationServiceTest (new): service-level unit tests

Closes #28 (backend slice)

Co-authored-by: copilot-autopilot[bot] <copilot-autopilot[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve AVAudio channel count mismatch crash on launch

Pass explicit mono AVAudioFormat when connecting player node to
mainMixerNode instead of nil. Passing nil caused the engine to
inherit the stereo output format while buffers were mono (1ch),
triggering the 'channelCount == buffer.format.channelCount' assertion.

commit.message:
Fix cross-realm prototype resolution for Map/Set constructors

When Reflect.construct(Map, [], ChildRealmCtor) is used, the prototype
should come from the child realm's Map.prototype, not the parent's.

Two fixes:
1. Function constructor: check new_target's __realm_id__ to set correct
   dynamic_origin_global for child realm functions
2. default_intrinsic_prototype_for_constructor: when origin_global is a
   child realm and constructor is a bare VmNativeFunction, read prototype
   from child VM's native function properties instead of parent's

Fixes: Map/proto-from-ctor-realm.js, Set/proto-from-ctor-realm.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make v2 (clean) the default index, keep v1 as /v1

- Renamed index.astro → v1.astro (GSAP animated version)
- Renamed v2.astro → index.astro (clean section-based version)
- v2 is now the default landing page at /

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove verbose UADE file-lookup logging from WASM

The uade_find_amiga_file and uade_request_amiga_file functions logged every
file resolution step to stderr, which in the browser produces full WASM
stack traces for each log line. For ZoundMonitor with 16 samples, this
flooded the console with 8000+ lines.

Keep error-path logging (file not found, fopen failed) for debugging.
Remove success-path verbose traces.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: FT2 toolbar visualizer clipped to black line

The responsive fix (84af2496) added overflow-hidden to the VisualizerFrame's
outer className, which clipped the 100px-tall visualizer content to the
~68px height of the adjacent toolbar rows. Removed overflow-hidden from the
Tailwind classes — the .viz-frame CSS already has its own overflow: hidden
for content clipping, and flex-1 + min-w-0 still prevents overlap on narrow
windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
